perturbation.original_text: 2. Confidential Informatio n. “Confidential Information” means any information , including investigator’s brochures, case report forms and protocols, disclosed by or on behalf of Disclosure identified as confidential when first disclosed and provided in tangible form, or if disclosed orally summarized in a writing pro vided by the Discloser to Institution within twenty (20) days after oral disclosure, other than information that: (a) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by In stitution; (b) is already known by or in the possessio n of Institution at the time of disclosure by Discloser; (c) is indepen dently developed by In stitution witho ut use of or refere nce to Discl oser’s Confidential Information; or (d) is obtained by Institution from a third party that has not breached any obligations of confident iality.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Confidential Informatio n. “Confidential Information” means only investigator’s brochures disclosed by Disclosure regardless if identified as confidential or in tangible form, and even if disclosed orally without being summarized in writing, and includes all information regardless if it: (a) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by In stitution; (b) is already known by or in the possessio n of Institution at the time of disclosure by Discloser; (c) is indepen dently developed by In stitution witho ut use of or refere nce to Discl oser’s Confidential Information; or (d) is obtained by Institution from a third party that has not breached any obligations of confident iality.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in the definition of 'Confidential Information'. The original text specifies that information must be 'identified as confidential when first disclosed and provided in tangible form' to be considered confidential. The modified version broadens the definition by stating it includes all information regardless if it is 'identified as confidential or in tangible form' or 'summarized in writing'. This contradicts the original requirement, creating uncertainty about what information the Institution is actually obligated to protect. Now the text, under the new definition, is including what originally should not be included, that is, not confidential under the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3 Care. Institution shall protect Discloser’s Confidential Information using not less than the same care it uses with respect to its own confidential information, but at all times at least reasonable care.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires Confidential Information to be 'identified as confidential when first disclosed and provided in tangible form'. The changed text broadens the definition to include information regardless of being 'identified as confidential or in tangible form' or 'summarized in writing'. This creates a direct conflict because the contradicted text, Section 3.3, states Institution shall protect Discloser’s Confidential Information. The change now forces the institution to protect information that was not initially intended to be protected as Confidential Information, leading to an in-text contradiction. The change alters the scope of what is considered Confidential Information, making the original agreement regarding the protection of it contradictory to the new definition.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 Term. This Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date, and continue for a perio d of one (1) year, or until earlier terminated by one party providing written notice of termination to the othe r, whichever comes first.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 Term. This Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date, and continue indefinitely, unless terminated by mutual agreement of both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement specifies a term of one year with the possibility of earlier termination via written notice. The modified version states the agreement continues indefinitely unless terminated by mutual agreement. This directly contradicts the initial term and the unilateral termination clause. This change will create uncertainty about the duration of the agreement, especially related to Confidentiality that has in 5.2 a survival clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.2 Survival . Institution's duty to protect Discloser’s Confidential Information shall survive th e termination or expiration of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined a one-year term with unilateral termination. The changed text makes the agreement indefinite, terminable only by mutual agreement. This directly contradicts the original term and termination clause. Section 5.2 states that Institution's duty to protect Discloser’s Confidential Information shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years. The change in 5.1 from 'one (1) year' or 'earlier terminated' to 'indefinitely, unless terminated by mutual agreement' directly affects the survival clause in 5.2. If the contract continues indefinitely then the survival clause is meaningless, but if the contract is terminated, the survival clause is triggered for 5 years after termination.

perturbation.original_text: 5.3 Return or Destroy. Institution shall promptly return o r, at Discloser’s option, destr oy, all Discloser’s Confidential Information related to a clinical study if Institution does not participate in such clinical study, upon termination or expiration of this Agreement, or upon written req uest by th e Discl oser, provided however, that Institution may retain in confidence under this Agreement one archived copy of the Discloser’s Confidential Information solely for the purpose of administering In stitution’s obligations hereun der.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.3 Return or Destroy. Institution may, at its sole discretion, retain all Discloser’s Confidential Information related to a clinical study, even if Institution does not participate in such clinical study, regardless of termination or expiration of this Agreement, or written request by the Discloser. The institution is not obliged to keep any archived copies.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause requires the Institution to return or destroy Confidential Information under certain conditions, with a provision for retaining one archived copy. The modified clause grants the Institution complete discretion to retain all information regardless of the circumstances, contradicting the obligation to return or destroy and the limitations on retaining copies. This affects ownership and what is expected to be return, as stated in Section 4. Ownership o f Confidential Informatio n. Institution agrees that Discloser shall retain all rights to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Ownership o f Confidential Informatio n. Institution agrees that Discloser shall retain all rights to the Confidential Information. No license of any such rights to Institution is granted or implied.
perturbation.justification: The original clause 5.3 mandated the Institution to return or destroy Confidential Information, while the changed clause grants the Institution the discretion to retain all information, even against the Discloser's request. This directly contradicts section 4, which states that "Discloser shall retain all rights to the Confidential Information," because the Institution's ability to keep the information at its discretion infringes upon the Discloser's ownership rights. This creates a strong in-text contradiction. The Perturbation Type is Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction and aligns with the definition that structural flaws can cause in-text contradictions when a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: 3.2 Non-Disclosure. Institution agrees not to disclose or ot herwise make available any of the Confidential Information to anyone except those employees and agents of Institution who need to know the Confidential Information to carry out the Purpose and who are bound by obligations of non- use and non -disclosure substantially similar to those set forth herein . Institution shall be responsible for any disclosure or use of the Confidential Information by its employees or agents.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 Non-Disclosure. Institution agrees to disclose Confidential Information to anyone including parties outside the Institution without obligations of non- use and non -disclosure. Institution shall not be responsible for any disclosure or use of the Confidential Information by its employees or agents.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause strictly prohibits disclosure of Confidential Information except to specific employees and agents under similar confidentiality obligations, holding the Institution responsible for their actions. The modified version allows disclosure to anyone, including external parties, without any confidentiality requirements, and absolves the Institution of responsibility for their actions. This is a direct contradiction of the non-disclosure obligation and responsibility, therefore affects the clause that talks about damages for this type of breach. Injunctive Relief. Institutio n ag rees that (i) any breach of this Agreement may result in significant and irreparable damage to Discloser, and (ii) Discloser shall be entitled, in addition to any other remedies ava ilable at law, to seek injunct ive or other equitable relief by a c ourt o f appropri ate jurisdic tion in th e event of any breach of this Agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Injunctive Relief. Institutio n ag rees that (i) any breach of this Agreement may result in significant and irreparable damage to Discloser, and (ii) Discloser shall be entitled, in addition to any other remedies ava ilable at law, to seek injunct ive or other equitable relief by a c ourt o f appropri ate jurisdic tion in th e event of any breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 3.2 directly contradicts section 6 (Injunctive Relief). The original text and the contradicted text both state that a breach of the agreement may result in irreparable damage. However, the changed text completely absolves the institution from responsibility for any disclosure or use of confidential information, effectively negating the possibility of a breach and therefore making the injunctive relief clause in section 6 unenforceable. The change allows disclosure to anyone without confidentiality requirements which creates a conflict with the remedy for any breach.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 Use. Institution agrees to u se the Confidential Information only for evaluation purposes to determine whether to conduct clinical studies sponsored by Discloser . Institution shall not use the Confidential Information for its own b enefit or the benefit of another.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 Use. Institution may use the Confidential Information for any purpose, including its own benefit or the benefit of another, without restriction.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause restricts the use of Confidential Information to evaluation purposes for potential clinical studies sponsored by the Discloser, prohibiting its use for the Institution's own benefit or the benefit of others. The modified version removes all restrictions, allowing the Institution to use the information for any purpose, including commercial gain. This directly contradicts the purpose and limitation on the use of the confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Background . From time to time during the term of this Agreement, Discloser may disclose to Ins titution certain confidential information concerning Discloser’s clinical studies for the purpose of permitting Institution to evaluate whether to conduct clinical studies sponsored by Discloser (the Purpose”). A general description of the confidential information to be disclosed is attached as Appendix A.
perturbation.justification: The original text limits the institution's use of confidential information to evaluation purposes for potential clinical studies sponsored by the Discloser. The changed text removes these restrictions, allowing the institution to use the information for any purpose. This directly contradicts the background stated in the contradicted text, which explicitly states the purpose of disclosing confidential information is solely 'for the purpose of permitting Institution to evaluate whether to conduct clinical studies sponsored by Discloser'. The changed text now allows for uses beyond this defined purpose, creating a direct and impactful contradiction and a structural flaw in the contract.

perturbation.original_text: 7.1 No Obligation. This Agreement does not obligate the parties to enter into negotiations or any subsequent agreement. Any agreement concerning a relationship can only be made in a definitive written agreement, executed on behalf of each party by an authorized representative .
perturbation.changed_text: 7.1 No Obligation. This Agreement automatically obligates the parties to enter into negotiations for any subsequent agreement immediately upon the termination of the contract and any agreement concerning a relationship can be made verbally.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that the agreement does not obligate the parties to enter into negotiations for any subsequent agreement and any agreement has to be made in a definitive written agreement, executed on behalf of each party by an authorized representative. The modified version completely changes the meaning. By stating that: This Agreement automatically obligates the parties to enter into negotiations for any subsequent agreement immediately upon the termination of the contract and any agreement concerning a relationship can be made verbally. By doing this, the meaning of that clause is changing completely the the clause, and creating uncertainty about the obligations
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.2 Entire Agreement. This Agreement is the entire agreement between the parties relating to the subject matter hereof and supersedes a ll prior agreements between the parties relating to the subject matter hereof. No agreement modifying or waiving any provision of this Agreement shall be binding unless made in a writing that references this Agreement and is signed by the part ies. Facsimile or electronic signed copies shall have the same effect as originals.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement does not obligate the parties to enter into negotiations and that any agreement has to be in writing. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that the agreement automatically obligates negotiations upon termination and that agreements can be made verbally. This directly contradicts section 7.2, which states that the agreement is the entire agreement and that no modifications are binding unless in writing.
